rationale: Both are grounded and free of fabrication, but Y engages the actual review threads: it explicitly discusses the performance/accuracy A/B evidence (matching Gaohan123's perf-comparison ask) and cites the non-Qwen FLUX.1-dev validation that resolved hsliuustc0106's scope concern about the global default change — the two biggest substantive threads in the real review. X validates test coverage thoroughly and gives more concrete, line-anchored suggestions (docstring note, defensive comment, assert addition), but its findings are largely novel nits unrelated to what reviewers actually flagged, missing the scope-justification and performance-comparison threads entirely. Neither catches Gaohan123's 'pytest mark is not complete' comment. Y's higher recall of the true concerns outweighs X's edge in actionability specificity.
